ety: cf. S. अङ्गार
def: s.m. Live coal; charcoal (also written zag̠ār).
s.m. The early part of the forenoon (after sunrise, i.e. the brightest part of the day); breakfast-time
more: (from ضحو To be exposed to the sun. Heb. To be white or shining) s. m. Breakfast-time, that part of the day, about half-way between sunrise and meridian (i. e. the brightest part of the day). عيد الضحا īﻌdu-ẓ-ẓuḥā, s. f. Name of a festival celebrated on the 10th of ẕī-ḥijja, when an animal is sacrificed in commemoration of Abrahamʼs sacrifice of his son Ismaʼīﻌl, according to the Musalmāns.
subs: s.f. 'The festival of the victims' ( = ʻīduʼl-aẓḥā), a festival celebrated on the tenth of ẕī-ḥijja, when an animal is sacrificed in commemoration of Abraham's sacrifice of his son Ishmael, according to the Musalmāns.

def: s.m. The early part of the forenoon (after sunrise, i.e. the brightest part of the day); breakfast-time
subs: s.f. 'The festival of the victims' ( = ʻīduʼl-aẓḥā), a festival celebrated on the tenth of ẕī-ḥijja, when an animal is sacrificed in commemoration of Abraham's sacrifice of his son Ishmael, according to the Musalmāns.

def: s.m. The planet Saturn.

ety: inf. n. of زهد 'to abstain from'
def: s.m. Abstinence; continence; devotion
more: s. m. Continence, devotion, abstinence. زهد کيش zuhd-kesh, adj. Devout, continent.

def: a male given name
s.m. pl. (of zāhid, q.v.), Religious men, devotees.
more: s. plur. of زاهد zāhid, q. v.

def: lunch, lunchtime meal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُهْرَانَه (zuhrāna). By surface analysis, ظُہْر (zuhr, “midday; noon; dhuhr”) + ـَانَہ (āna).

ety: inf. n. of ذهل 'to forget'
def: s.m. Forgetting, oblivion; neglecting, leaving (cf. ẕahl).

ety: inf. n. of ظہر 'to appear,' &c.
def: s.m. Appearing, arising, springing up; appearance, manifestation, visibility; coming to pass
subs: v.n. To become apparent or manifest, to appear, to arise, to come to pass, to present itself
v.t. To cause to appear, to render visible, to bring to pass, bring about
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُهُور (zuhūr). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi ظہور (z̤hvr).

def: n. m. 1. Accruing or arising (as a cause of action). 2. Manifestation; appearance; coming to pass. zuhūr bināë dāvā . Cause of action. zuhūr meṅ ānā , v. n. To arise ( udaë h. , and bītnā , 2). zuhūr meṅ lānā , v. a. To render visible; cause to appear; bring about. zahūr h., yā pānā , v. n. 1. See zāhir honā . 2. To arise; appear. 3. To come to be; come into existence.
more: s. m. Appearing, arising, manifestation, visibility, coming to pass.

def: 1. Auspiciousness; grace. Āp hī kā zahūrā hai! 2. Light; life; ornament. 3. ( Slang ) A son.

ety: corr. fr. A. z̤uhūr
def: s.m. (colloq.) Auspicious appearance, auspiciousness, grace; glory, fame; light; ornament.
v. n. (dakh.) To become manifest, to appear.

ety: v.n. fr. زجّ 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.m. Glass; a glass, or cup; a glass flask or bottle.

ety: fr. ذکو 'to flame, blaze'
def: s.m. The sun.

ety: v.n. fr. زکم
def: s.m. Catarrh, rheum, defluxion, a cold in the head
more: n. m. Rheum; catarrh; a cold in the head. zukām bigaṛnā , v. n. 1. To become thick and clotted (rheum). 2. To be suddenly stopped (the flow of rheum). zukām h. , v. n. To take cold in the head. Meṅḍkī ko bhī zukām huā . Prov. The frog too has a cold! (A sailor, and afraid of the sea!).
(from زکم) s. m. Rheum, defluxion, catarrh, a cold.
subs: v.n. The defluxion from the head to be suddenly stopped; the rheum to become thick and clotted
(-ko), To have or take a cold.

def: s. (dakh.) Catarrh, a cold ( Harris ): see زکام zukām.
s.m. = ẕaḵẖīra, q.v. s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
more: s. m. Treasure, provisions, victuals, stores, munition.
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

def: s.m. pl. of ẕakar, q.v.; and see next.

ety: for A. ذکورة ẕukūrat, pl. of ẕakar
def: s.m. Males; the male sex.

ety: prob. the A. dim. of زلخا, fem. of ازلخ; rt. زلخ 'to slip'
def: s.f. Name of Potiphar's wife (whose passion for Joseph is much celebrated in the East, particularly in the elegant Persian poems by Nazāmī and Jāmī).
more: n. prop. The name of Potipharʼs wife.

ety: v.n. fr. زلّ 'to slip'; 'to run,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Cool, cold, sweet, pure, or limpid (water); — pure water, &c.
more: adj. زل moving. pure; clear (water). Āb-i-zulāl .
(from زلّ) adj. Pure, limpid, wholesome (water); s. m. Water of that quality.

def: s.m. The cholic.

ety: said to be deriv. fr. A. zulaf, pl. of zulfat, 'a portion of the first part of the night'; but prob. akin to S. गुल्म, or गुम्फ
def: s.f. A curling lock (hanging down upon the temple or over the ear), side-lock, curl, ringlet, lock, tress; tuft; knot; whisker
more: n. f. A lock of hair; love lock; curl; ringlet; tresses. Julaf us ke chehre pe bal khā rahī hai, Ki jūṅ nāṅd meṅ bhaiṅs khal khā rahī hai . The curls about her face are curved and bent, As buffalo stoops upon oil-cakes intent. (A satire on the poetical similes of rustics). ham-zulf ; H. sāṛhū , n. m. The wife's sister's husband.

s. f. A curling lock, a ringlet, whisker. زلف پريشان zulf-i-pareshān, Dishevelled locks. زلف تابدار zulf-i-tāb-dār, Curling locks. زلف دراز zulf-i-darāz, Long locks. زلف عنبرين zulf-i-aﻌmbarīn, Perfumed (with ambergris) locks.
s. f. A side lock (of hair).
subs: s.f. Locks perfumed with ambergris, perfumed locks.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زُلْف (zulf, “hair, specially if dishevelled”). See the Persian entry for more.

def: direct plural of زُلْف (zulf)

ety: zulf+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ; or contrac. fr. P. zulfīn
def: s.f. A sword-knot; — the chain by which a door is fastened; ring (of a door), bolt.
more: n. f. 1. A door chain to keep the door from being lifted from its place; chain hinges. 2. A sword-knot.
s. m. 1. A swordknot. 2. The chain by which a door is fastened.

def: oblique plural of زُلْف (zulf)
Dhu'l-Hijja (twelfth and final month of the Islamic calendar, during which the hajj takes place)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذوالحجه (zūlhijja), from Arabic ذُو الحِجَّة (ḏū l-ḥijja). By surface analysis, Arabic ذُو (ḏū, “possessed of”) + Arabic الحِجَّة (al-ḥijja, “the hajj”).

def: Dhu'l-Kifl (a prophet of Islam, sometimes identified with Ezekiel in the Bible); a male given name from Arabic
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذُوالْکِفْل (zū-l-kifl), from Arabic ذُو الْكِفْل (ḏū l-kifl).

ety: inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).
more: (Heb. and Ch. ) s. m. Baseness, meanness. ¶ ẕill, s. m. Gentleness, ease.

ety: for A. ظلة, v.n. fr. ظلّ 'to shade,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing that covers, or shades, or protects; covering; a cloud.
more: s. m. A covering, any thing shady, a cloud.

ety: inf. n. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully,' &c.
def: s.m. Wrong, wrong-doing, injustice, oppression, tyranny; exaction, extortion; violence, outrage, injury; grievance, hardship; a heavy burden, a heavy assessment
more: n. m. 1. Oppression; extortion; tyranny. 2. Violence; force; outrage. 3. Grievance; duress; hardship. 4. A heavy assessment. zulm bar zāt-i-khās . Private injury or wrong. zulm-pesha, zulm-shāar, zulm-kesh , Poet. adj. Tyrannical. zulm-rasīdah, mazlūm , adj. Oppressed. zulm se , adv. Unjustly; tyrannically. zulm-i-sarīh . Flagrant injury; gross injustice. zulm-i-ām . A public wrong. zulm k. , v. a. 1. To oppress; tyrannize. 2. To wrong; injure; extort. zulm o bidat se , adv. Vexatiously. zulm o sitam, yā tāaddī , n. f. Maltreatment.

s. m. Oppression, injustice, tyranny, injury. ظلم رسيده z̤ulm-rasīda, adj. Oppressed. ظلم پيشه z̤ulm-pesha, or ظلم شعار z̤ulm-shiʼāﻌr, or ظلم کيش z̤ulm-kesh, adj. Tyrannical; tyrant, oppressor. ظلم کرنا z̤ulm karnā, To oppress, tyrannise. ظلم گدازي z̤ulm-gudāz-ī, s. f. Dissolving of oppresion, the putting an end to injustice.
subs: adj. & s.m. Practising tyranny, tyrannical; — one whose business is tyranny, a tyrant, an oppressor
adj. & s.m. Oppressed; — one who is oppressed, a victim of tyranny (syn. maz̤lūm)
(-par), To practise oppression or tyranny (on), to oppress, to tyrannize (over), to do wrong (to), to wrong, injure; to practise extortion or exaction (on)
adj. & s.m. = z̤ulm-shěʻār, and z̤ulm-pesha, q.v.
s.f. 'Dissolving of oppression,' putting an end to oppression or injustice.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُلْم (zulm), borrowed from Arabic ظُلْم (ẓulm).

def: persecution; tyranny, oppression
wety: From Urdu ظلم (“oppression”) + Urdu ستم (“tyranny”).

def: adj. Dark, obscure.

ety: for A. ظلمة z̤ulmat
def: s.f. Darkness, obscurity.

ety: rel. n. fr. z̤ulm
def: adj. Unjust, addicted to tyranny, oppressive, tyrannical.

ety: for A. ظلمية; z̤ulm+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Iniquity, oppression, tyranny.
s.f. (pl. of z̤ulmat), Darkness, regions of darkness; a dark place where the water of immortality is said to be.
more: n. f. pl. of ظلمت Darkness; regions of darkness, where the water of immortality is said to be. bahr-i-zulmāt, bahr-i-uqyānūs . The Atlantic ocean.
s. f. 1. (Plur. of ظلمت) Darkness. 2. A dark place where the water of immortality is said to be.

ety: for A. زمرة, v.n. fr. زمر 'to pipe,' &c.
def: s.m. Company, body, troop, legion, crowd, multitude, number (of persons).
more: n. m. A company; troop. Yeh bhī to bāgiyoṅ ke zumre meṅ the .
(from زمر Heb. To sing, to play on a flute, &c.: and, perhaps, so named from its buzzing noise) s. m. A troop, a group, crowd, multitude.

ety: prob. = S. स्तम्भकः
def: adj. Styptic, astringent (taste or flavour, &c.; — syn. kaselā).

ety: the arabicized form of P. zumrud = Zend aśmareja? or mareja?; S. मरकतः
def: s.m. The emerald.

ety: zumurrud, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of the colour of an emerald.
more: adj. Of the colour of an emerald.

def: n. Cor. of zul-mānain . A pun; a joke; a double entendre. zū-funūn' , An artful person.
adj. (dakh.) Fop, beau: see بانکا bānkā.
wety: Borrowed from English zone.

ety: prob. fr. Gr. ζωνάρη, or ζωνάριον
def: adj. & s.m. Belted, having on the sacred thread; — one who wears the sacred thread; — a Brāhman
more: n. m. The sacred thread ( janeū ). Dhotī bibīṅ! pothī bibīṅ! dar gulū zunnār! Islām qulī pāṅḍe manam, pūriāṅ biyār! Loins-strip behold! my book behold! and eke the sacred string! A Moslem to a Pānḍa turned! your crisp sweet cakes forth bring! ☞ The story runs that a Mahomedan mendicant being refused any of the dainty food on which some Hindus were feasting, on the ground that he was not of their faith, forthwith changed his attire and came before them in the garb of a Hindu priest. zunnār-dār . One who wears the sacred thread; a Brāhman.
s. f. A belt, the brahmanical thread, a Hindū rosary. (From Gr. ζωνὰριον ).
subs: s.f. Investiture with the Brāhmanical thread.

def: s.m. pl. (of ẕamb), Sins, crimes, misdeeds, &c.
s.m. pl. (of z̤ann, q.v.), Thoughts, opinions, &c.
more: s. m. 1. Suspecting. 2. (plur. of ظنّ) Thoughts, suspicions.

ety: inf. n. of ظنّ; see z̤ann
def: s.m. Thinking, supposing, suspecting.

ety: v.n. fr. زور 'to incline,' &c.
def: s.m. Lie, falsehood, untruth; adulteration; — a false deity, an idol; — hypocrisy, dissimulation, deceit.
more: n. m. زور a lie. 1. A false gloss or coloring. 2. Hypocrisy; dissimulation. Shaitān bhī ādmī hai jo kartā hai makr o zūr, Aur hādī rah-numā hai so hai voh bhī ādmī ! Nazīr. And Satan too is man whose work's deceit and lure, The priest who shows the way is also man, be sure!
s. m. 1. A lie, a falsehood, adulteration. 2. A false deity, an idol. ¶ zaur, s. m. Going on a pilgrimage.
wety: From Classical Persian زور (zōr, “strength, force; power, might”).

ety: for A. ذرارة ẕurārat, v.n. fr. ذرّ 'to scatter, sprinkle'
def: s.m. Particles flying or scattered about of anything grinding or pounding.
more: (from ذرّ To grind, to powder. Heb. To scatter) s. m. Particles scattered from any thing pounding.

def: Visit rarely.

ety: for A. ذرية, fr. ذرّ 'to scatter, spread,' &c.
def: s.f. Progeny, offspring, children, little ones, young ones, descendants, race, stock, seed.
s.f. pl. (of ẓarb, q.v.), Blows, &c.; modes of speech.
more: s. plur. of ضرب ẓarb, q. v.

def: s.m. pl. (of z̤arf), Vessels; — adverbs
subs: China vessels or ware, china, porcelain
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُرُوف (zurūf), from Arabic ظُرُوف (ẓurūf)

def: s.m. Zedoary, Curcuma zerumbet.
more: n. m. An ornamental gourd; zedoary; curcuma zerumbet .
s. Name of a plant, zedoary. (Curcuma zerumbet: Roxb. See As. Res. vol. xi. ).

def: a mistranscription for زيادة ziyādat, q.v.
The thirty-fifth letter of the Urdu alphabet, called ہَمْزَہ (hamza, “Hamza”) and written in the Arabic script.

n. m. 1. Engagement; stipulation (اقرلر 1, 3). 2. See zamānah , 1, 2. 3. Reign ( rāj 1, 5). ahd toṛnā , v. a. To break one's promise or engagement. ahd ṭūṭnā , v. n. To be broken (a contract). ahd-i-hukūmat . In the reign of. ahd-shikan , adj. Faithless to one's word or engagement. ahd-shiknī , n. f. Breach of faith, contract, or engagement; infraction of a treaty. ahd k. , v. a. 1. See iqrār k. , 1, 4. 2. To abjure. 3. To make a vow. ahd karvānā , v. a. To bind; make conditions. ahd meṅ , adv. In the reign of. ahd-nāmah , n. m. A convention; charter; treaty ( iqrār-nāma ). ahd-nāma-i-lijārat . A commercial treaty. ahd-nāmah likhvānā . To bind; oblige by contract. ahd o paimān , n. m. Articles of agreement; treaty of alliance. ahd o paimān k. , v. a. To enter into, or make a convention; make a treaty; make terms or conditions ( iqrār k. , 4). ahd o paimān karne-vāle . The contracting parties.
more: s. m. f. 1. Contract, compact, obligation, promise, treaty. 2. Time, season, conjuncture. عـﮩد شکن aﻌhd-shikan, Promise-breaker, faithless to oneʼs word or engagement. عـﮩد شکني aﻌhd-shikan-ī, s. f. Promise-breaking, breach of faith. عـﮩد کرنا aﻌhd karnā, To promise, to vow. عـﮩد و پيمان کرنا aﻌhd o paimān karnā, To covenant, to bargain. عـﮩد نامه aﻌhd-nāma, A contract, an agreement in writing, charter, articles of peace, &c.

def: n. m. A sluggard. See احدي Hil-ke pānī tak, ajī, pīte nahīṅ, Tum to ahdī se sivā majhul ho! Wom.
adj. 1. Like (اکساں). Yeh donoṅ ain gain haiṅ, ise chhupāo, use nikālo . 2. ( lit . the difference of a dot) Squint-eyed. 3. Having a cataract.

n. m. pl. of عجيت Wonders; curiosities; lions. ajāib, ajāib o garāib , adj. Wonderful; extraordinary. ajāëbāt-i-zamānah . The wonders of the world. ajāib-ul-makhlūqāt , n. m. 1. The wonders of the creation. 2. Monsters; lusus naturae. ajāyab-khānah, ajāib-ghar , n. m. A museum.

n. f. 1. Inability ( bintī ). 2. Humility ( inkisār ). 1. Ājizī sab ko pyārī hai . Prov. Saying. Humility is pleasing to all. 2. Ajizī se kām nikālo . With humility attain your ends. 3. Supplication; helplessness. ājizī k. , v. a. To press eagerly; to supplicate. Bahuterī ājizī kī, par ek na mānī .
n. f. Severalty; separation.

adj. and adv. Separate (الگ adj., 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 10—12, 14, and alag , adv. 1, 2). alahdah rakhnā , v. a. To appropriate; set apart; segregate. alahdah alahdah , adv. Separately; one by one; apiece. alahdah k. , v. a. To separate (الگ 1, 2, 4-9 12-16, 18.). alahdah h. , v. n. To be separated ( alag h .). māl alahdā k. , To part with a property.
On them.
On him (be) the mercy (of God)!
God bless him.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلَیْهِ السَّلَام (alayh-i s-salām), from Arabic عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ (ʕalayhi s-salāmu).

def: A proceeding; process. amal-dar-āmad k. , v. a. To act upon; observe. amal-dar-āmad h. , v. n. To be acted upon. amal-dastak . Warrant; writ; a deed of conveyance; a certificate of title. amal k., yā amal meṅ lānā , v. a. 1. To put in execution; carry into effect; bring into operation ( bajā lānā ). 2. To act; set to work; transact (business). 3. To use ( istemāl k. , 1 and Phr ., also baratnā , 1). 4. To dispense; administer. 5. To effectuate ( asar k . 1). 6. See amal pānī k. davā kā amal badalnā , v. a. To change the operation of a drug.
adj. Of the color or odour of ambergris.
n. m. Ministerial officer clerks; officials; executive authority.
n. m. John Doe and Richard Roe (زيد).
more: Fictitious names, like John Nokes and Thomas Stiles, used in scholastick or juridical treatises.

def: adv. عن prepos. and قريب 1. Adjoining; adjacent; bordering upon ( pās , 1). 2. Very soon; shortly; nearly; almost. 3. ( anqarīb ānevālā ) About to happen; approaching; imminent; impending; threatening.
more: (عن prepos. and قريب Near) adv. Near, soon, shortly, nearly, almost.

def: v. a. Properly spelt with alif . To applaud; praise. Uskā joṛā dekh-kar sab ash ash karne lage . Wom.
more: To rejoice, to be greatly pleased or contented.

def: The sacrament. ashāë rabbānī ; H. pirbhū bhoj . The Lord's supper.
n. m. 1. Og, king of Bashan. 2. A tall foolish man.

adj. عين the eye. Visible; distinct; conspicuous; clear (ظاﮩر). Ayāṅ rā che bayāṅ? P. Prov. What's plain as day what need to say. ayāṅ k. , v. a. To throw light upon; lay bare or open; unfold; elucidate ( praghaṭ k .).
wety: From Arabic عَيَّان (ʕayyān).

def: n. m. pl. اعضم q. v. Member; limb. azv-i-tanāsul , n. m. Membrum virile . azv-shikanī . Mutilation; maiming. azv kā kāṭnā yā bekār k. , v. a. 1. To cut off a limb. 2. To mutilate.

A return; commutation (ادل بدل). Evaz māvaz gilā na dārad . P. Prov. One thing for another is complaint's demurrer. (Exchange is no robbery). evaz māvaz k. , v. a. To interchange ( adlā badlā k . 1). evaz meṅ h. , v. n. To represent; replace; supersede; supplant ( badlī meṅ h .). evaz meṅ, ba-evaz , 1. As compensation or indemnity; in satisfaction. 2. Muṭaṭis mutandis ; in supercession of. evaz-i-nuqsān, tāvān , H. ṭoṭe kī bharautī, ḍanḍ , Indemnity; indemnification. ba-evaz qīmat-i-muaiyan . G. G. At a certain price. evazānā kājī . Sufficient amends.

n. m. Office; duty; occupation; charge ( jagah , 3). ohdah-barā h. , v. n. To discharge a duty; fulfil an engagement. ohdah-barāī , n. f. 1. Discharge of duty; completion of an engagement. 2. See barīyat. ohdah-barāī k. , v. a. 1. To acquit one's self of an obligation. 2. To discharge the duties of an office. ohdah pānā , v. n. To be appointed to an office, entrusted with a commission. ohde par māmūr, yā muqarrar k., ohdah denā , v. a. To appoint to, or instal in an office. ohda-i-jajī , G. G. The office of a judge. ohdah-i-jalīl . An honorable or high place, office, or dignity. ohdeh-dār . An office-holder; officer; functionary; civil or military authority. ohdeh-dār-i-kharch-kunindah . The disbursing officer. ohdeh-dār-i-sarkārī , n. A government official; a public officer. ohdeh-dār-i-mātahat . A subordinate officer. ohdeh-dār-i-māfauq, yā ālā A superior officer. ohdeh-dārī . Holding an office; incumbency. ohdah rakhnā . To hold or fill an office. ohde se bartaraf, yā māzūl h. , v. n. To be dismissed from an office. ohdeh se dast-bar-dār h. , v. n. See istīfā denā. ohda-i-āmil . An executive authority. ohda-i-qāyam-muqāmī . An officiating appointment. ohde ke etabār se, ba-etabār-i-ohdah . By virtue of one's office; ex officio. kisī ohde ke hīle se . G. G. Under color of any office. masrūf ba-kār-i-ohdah ; G. G. H. kām par . On duty.
more: s. m. 1. A commission, an obligation, an agreement. 2. Business, office, post, appointment. (vulg. hudda ). عـﮩده برآ uﻌhda-bar-ā, adj. Performing an engagement or being able to accomplish an undertaking, successful. عـﮩده براٴي uﻌhda-bar-ā-ī, s. f. Completion of an agreement, ability to perform any work or obligation, performance, success. عـﮩده دار uﻌhda-dār, Intrusted with a business, employed, holding a commission, an officer. عـﮩدي سي برآنا uﻌhde se bar ānā, or عـﮩدي سي نکلنا uﻌhde se nikalnā, To be able to perform an engagement or business, to accomplish a business, to succeed in an undertaking.

def: n. m. A fabulous bird.
adj. Naked; bare.
Gamboge.
vao with hamza, see ء.
ye with hamza, see ء.
A negating prefix. non-, un-, a-, an-, etc.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अ- (a-).

def: hereupon
hereto, hereunto, herewith
wherefore, therefore

s.f. Invention, &c. ( = اکت ukat, q.v.). s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

ety: old P. awar; Pehl. apar; Zend upairi; S. उपरि = H. ūpar; par
def: prep. & prefix. On, upon, up, above; at; in, into; with; forth, back, away, against; based upon, according to; on account of. (Prefixed to words it modifies or strengthens the sense, as will appear from the numerous examples which will be found in their proper alphabetical places.)
Bethlehem (a city in the West Bank, Palestine)
wety: From Arabic بَيْت لَحْم (bayt laḥm), ultimately from Hebrew בֵּית לֶחֶם (bet lékhem).

def: alternative form of بَیت اَلْلَحَم (bait allaham)
International Phonetic Alphabet

ety: पैंताना [paiṅtānā, पेंताना peṅtānā [Prk. पायंतअडअओ, or पयंतअडअओ; S. पाद (or पद)+अन्तक+र+कः
def: s.m. The foot part (of a bedstead, tomb, &c. = pāyantī).
more: s. m. The foot of a bed, the side towards the feet.

ety: caus. of taṛapnā; — ā˚ = āw˚ = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि
def: v.t. To render uneasy or restless; to put (one) in great agitation; to cause to flutter; to cause to stumble, &c. (see taṛapnā).
more: (causal of تڙپنا) v. a. 1. To put in great agitation. 2. To cause to flutter or stumble.

ety: S. तृष्
def: s.f. Thirst; wish, strong desire ( = tr̤ishā, q.v.).
ergative of تو (tū)
Thank you; many thanks.
wety: From Hebrew תּוֹדָה רַבָּה (todá rabá, “thank you very much”).

def: to be quiet, to be silent
wety: From خاموش (xāmoš, “silent”) + ہونا (honā, “to be”), literally “to be silent”.

def: overnight
may God have mercy on him
wety: Borrowed from Arabic رَحْمَةُ اللَّٰهِ عَلَيْه (raḥmatu llāhi ʕalayh).

def: Romania (a country in Southeast Europe)
There is no fully established single-word equivalent of slang in Urdu. The English word slang is now commonly used in Urdu as well.
wety: Borrowed from English slang.

def: to die
wety: From فناہ (fanā, “die”) + ہونا (honā, “to be”), literally “to die”.

ety: Prk. मम्मो; S. मर्म
def: s.m. Secret quality; latent power or virtue; power; resources, means; money.
believer, one who believes
wety: From ماننا (mannā, “to believe”) + والا (vālā, “one, who”).

def: alternative form of مجھے
wety: From Dehlavi Urdu.

def: yes sir, yes madam; yes
wety: From ہاں (hā̃) + جی (jī).

def: ergative of ہم (ham)
Another title for సామంత in certain dialects of Dekhanim.
